blurb:
This is the place we go when someone in the family is craving something completely different from everyone else. It's a classic neon-signed diner that's open 24/7, so whether you want pancakes at 2pm or a Greek salad at midnight, they've got you covered. Perfect for those nights when you can't decide what to eat or need somewhere reliable after a late movie.

The breakfast is huge and good any time of day, but don't sleep on their Greek dishes either. My kids love the pancakes and my wife always gets the gyro platter. The portions are generous, so come hungry or plan on taking leftovers home.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
One of my girlfriends dragged me here for a girls' night and I'm so glad she did. This is now officially on my top 5 dinner spots in Atlanta. The whole experience is just perfect, from the atmosphere to the service to the food. Great for date nights, group dinners, or celebrating something special.

Start with the hummus, then get the chicken shish kabobs with that rice that has lentils and raisins. The wings are surprisingly good too if you want something different. Save room for the tiramisu because it's worth it. Their cocktails are all really well made and not too sweet.

They break between lunch and dinner, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. But honestly, this place is worth planning your whole evening around.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is our go-to when we want a lot of food and don't want to think about what to order. It's all-you-can-eat Brazilian barbecue, which means they bring different grilled meats to your table until you wave the white flag. Perfect for celebrating something or when you have teenage boys to feed.

The cheese bread alone is worth the trip, and the feijoada stew is comfort food at its best. They also do breakfast, which is a nice touch if you want to try something different on the weekend. Just pace yourself because they keep bringing food until you tell them to stop.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This is where I go when I want to feel like I'm treating myself right. It's a bright, cheerful French bakery that makes everything from scratch, and you can taste the difference. Perfect for a weekend brunch or when you need to pick up something special for a dinner party.

The quiches are fantastic for lunch, and their baguettes are the real deal. Don't leave without trying the petits fours if you want something sweet. It's not cheap, but the quality is worth it, and the staff knows their stuff if you need help choosing.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
This is where I go when I'm craving proper Indian food without the fancy atmosphere or prices. It's in the Perimeter Center area, and while the decor is pretty basic, the food is legit. Perfect for family dinners or when you want to order way too much food for takeout.

The biryani is obviously what they're known for, and it comes in these huge portions that'll feed you for days. The family-style serving works great when you're with a group and want to try a bunch of different dishes. Everything comes out hot and flavorful, and they don't hold back on the spices.

Just know they stay open later on weekends, which is clutch when you get a late Indian food craving.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
This place absolutely lives up to all the hype you've probably heard. I was skeptical at first, but now I get why people rave about it. The atmosphere is vibrant without being loud, and the service is genuinely helpful. Perfect for girls' night out or when you want good Mexican that's not the usual chain stuff.

Start with the Esquite because it's packed with flavor and gets you in the mood for everything else. The Pineapple Guava pitcher is refreshing and strong enough to share, and the Passion Fruit one hits that perfect balance of sweet and boozy. Their tacos are obviously the star, and everything tastes fresh and authentic.

They're open until midnight most nights, which is clutch for late dinners or when you get off work and still want something good.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is the place my husband and I go for anniversaries or when we really want to celebrate something. It sits right on the Chattahoochee River, and if you can snag a table by the windows, the view is gorgeous. Perfect for date nights or impressing out-of-town guests.

The seafood is what they do best, especially anything with a Southern twist. My go-to is whatever fresh fish they're featuring, and their shrimp and grits never disappoints. The portions aren't huge, but everything is really well done. It's definitely a splurge, but worth it for special occasions.

Reservations are pretty much required, especially for weekend dinners. They do a nice Sunday brunch too if you want the same vibe but less formal.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
We discovered this place when we were looking for something different and healthy that wouldn't break the bank. It's casual and friendly, the kind of place where you order at the counter and they bring it to your table. Great for a quick lunch or when you want to try something new with the kids.

The shawarma is what keeps us coming back, and the falafel is crispy and fresh. They're good about explaining what everything is if you're new to Middle Eastern food, and there are plenty of vegetarian options too. The portions are generous for the price, which is a nice surprise these days.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
Finally, a pizza place that actually gets thin crust right. This is my new go-to when I want pizza that doesn't make me feel like I need a nap after. It's in the City Springs area, and the vibe is casual but not cheap feeling. Great for date nights or family dinners when everyone can agree on pizza.

Their traditional Italian style is legit, and they use ingredients imported from Italy which you can actually taste. The crust is crispy without being cardboard, and they don't overload it with toppings. I usually get something simple like margherita, but their antipasti are really good too if you want to start with something.

It gets busy on weekends, so either go early or be prepared to wait a bit. Worth it though.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is perfect when you're with a group that can't agree on what kind of food they want. It's a food hall with twenty different vendors all under one roof, so everyone can get exactly what they're craving. Great for families with picky eaters or when you want to try a few different things.

The variety is impressive and the quality is surprisingly good across the board. My wife and I like to share a few different dishes and make it more of a tasting experience. It gets busy on weekends, but that's part of the fun when you're walking around the square anyway.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM
